misconceptionSummary: Students calculate left to right regardless of operation, ignoring priority rules.

visuals.subText: Higher-priority operations are calculated first, regardless of position

steps.narration: BODMAS gives the order for mixed calculations. Brackets first, then powers and roots, then multiplication and division left to right, and finally addition and subtraction left to right.
In three plus four times two, multiplication comes before addition. Calculate four times two first to get eight, then add three to get eleven — not left to right, which would give fourteen.

Brackets change the order. With brackets around three plus four, the addition happens first: three plus four is seven, then seven times two is fourteen. Brackets override the default multiplication-first rule.

visuals.label: Same numbers, different order of operations — different answers

steps.narration: Eleven without brackets, fourteen with brackets. The order of operations determines the answer — always follow BODMAS, and use brackets to override when needed.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: 3 + 4 × 2 calculated left to right as 7 × 2 = 14. Multiplication takes priority over addition — calculate 4 × 2 = 8 first, then 3 + 8 = 11.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Left to right is not always right. Multiplication and division take priority over addition and subtraction — always check for higher-priority operations before working left to right.
